channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Alabama puts man to death for a 1991 murder in the nation's 4th execution using nitrogen gas
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama inmate convicted of murdering a woman after breaking into her apartment as she slept was put to death Thursday in the nation’s fourth execution using nitrogen gas. Demetrius Frazier, 52, was pronounced dead at 6:36 p.m.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Passengers restrain man who punched window on Frontier flight from Denver: Police
item.description: Passengers on a Frontier Airlines flight from Denver to Houston restrained a man after he punched a window and seat 20 minutes after takeoff.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Project 2025 architect Russell Vought to lead powerful White House budget office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Russell Vought as White House budget director on Thursday night, putting an official who has planned the zealous expansion of President Donald Trump's power into one of the most influential positions in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 13 states to sue over DOGE access to government payment systems containing personal data
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in several states vowed Thursday to file a lawsuit to stop Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing federal payment systems containing Americans' sensitive personal information.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration sues Chicago in latest crackdown on 'sanctuary' cities
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Trump administration sued Chicago on Thursday alleging that 'sanctuary' laws in the nation's third-largest city “thwart" federal efforts to enforce immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Jose State and Penn face inquiries in Trump crackdown on transgender athletes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is investigating potential civil rights violations at two universities and a high school sports league that allowed transgender athletes to compete on women's teams, the Education Department said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Gaza plan shocks the world but finds support in Israel
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to seek U.S. ownership of the Gaza Strip and move out its population infuriated the Arab world. It stunned American allies and other global powers and even flummoxed members of Trump’s own party. The reaction in Israel was starkly different.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE was tasked with stopping Treasury payments to USAID, AP sources say
item.description: Officials working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency sought access to the U.S. Department of Treasury payment system to stop money from flowing to the U.S. Agency for International Development, according to two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bond set at $20M for man accused in shooting at Ohio warehouse that killed 2 and wounded 4
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A warehouse worker said in a frantic 911 call that he was shot in the neck and then took the gun from a co-worker after he had shot several others. Two people died and four were injured in the Ohio shooting that sent panicked employees fleeing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Flying's gotten safer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Summoned from his couch to cover last week's plane disaster in Washington, CNN's Pete Muntean rushed in for the first of 24 live reports over the next 48 hours. At one point, he used a model airplane and helicopter to demonstrate.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Applebee’s brings back Date Night Passes
item.description: Applebee’s announced Thursday that it's bringing back the coveted Date Night Pass, which went viral on social media during its debut last year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NCAA changes transgender policy to limit women's competition to athletes assigned female at birth
item.description: The NCAA changed its participation policy for transgender athletes on Thursday, limiting competition in women's sports to athletes assigned female at birth only.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Daytona boss inspires students with his NASCAR journey
item.description: The "Super Bowl" of racing, the Daytona 500, will be the next biggest event in the world of sports, taking place February 16, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Davis Suppes, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge pauses Trump federal worker buyouts
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday extended the deadline for federal workers to accept the Trump administration’s sweeping buyout offer. The court will weigh the merits of the bid to block the offer in a Monday hearing.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Fentanyl with $3M estimated street value seized; Man in custody
item.description: Deputies arrested a North Carolina man after seizing nearly 7 pounds of fentanyl with a street value of $3 million.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Bright, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ sues Illinois, Chicago over ‘sanctuary city’ laws
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department sued the state of Illinois, the city of Chicago and local officials Thursday over laws creating a sanctuary city.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Crayola brings back 8 retired colors: Here's what they are
item.description: Crayola is going back to the drawing board with its latest collection, bringing back a special assortment of retired colors for the first time in its 122-year history.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, ABC 4

item.title: CIA shares new employee names through unclassified email
item.description: The nature of the communication risks compromising the identifies of the agents — sparking condemnation by leaders.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Freezing hikers stuck in waist-deep snow rescued during Mount Washington whiteout
item.description: The two friends had hiked New Hampshire's challenging Mount Washington trail a month ago. They returned and did it again Sunday after trekking through neighboring peaks in the White Mountains.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOGE officials home in on Medicare, Medicaid offices
item.description: It was unclear which systems DOGE had access to and whether any sensitive medical information was part of the efforts.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: 5 things to watch as the federal buyout deadline approaches
item.description: As the deadline approaches, agencies have amped up the pressure on employees, sending frequent “reminders” and a copy of the contract they would have to sign to accept the deferred resignation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FAA, NTSB to brief senators on Washington, DC midair collision
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration and National Transportation Safety Board are planning to brief senators just one week after a midair collision claimed the lives of 67 people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Second type of bird flu detected in US dairy cows
item.description: Dairy cattle in Nevada have been infected with a new type of bird flu that's different from the version that has spread in U.S. herds since last year, Agriculture Department officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Racial gap widened in deaths among US moms around the time of childbirth
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Black women in the U.S. died at a rate nearly 3.5 times higher than white women around the time of childbirth in 2023, as maternal mortality fell below prepandemic levels overall but racial gaps widened, according to federal health data released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexican troops begin arriving at US-Mexico border following deal made to pause Trump-approved tariffs
item.description: Mexico has started deploying soldiers to its northern border in accordance with the 10,000 troops it promised to send to the border under a deal made with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US seizes Venezuelan president's plane held in Dominican Republic during Rubio visit
item.description: The Trump administration has seized the second of two planes belonging to Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro's government that is currently in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Temple University police say 2 students, 1 former student impersonated ICE agents on campus
item.description: Two current students and one former student at Temple University were identified by campus police after allegedly impersonating U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Texas man is executed for the killing of a pastor during a robbery at a church
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas man convicted of beating and suffocating a Dallas area pastor in his church during a robbery was put to death Wednesday evening, the second execution in the U.S. this year and the first of four scheduled in Texas over the next three months.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police seek motive after a suspect was arrested for a fatal shooting at an Ohio cosmetics warehouse
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A man accused of shooting to death a coworker and wounding five others at an Ohio warehouse has spoken with police as they probe for a motive, authorities said Wednesday, hours after he was arrested in an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas to execute man who killed pastor in church days after being released from anger management program
item.description: Texas will put to death on Wednesday a man convicted of the brutal 2011 killing of a Dallas-area pastor in his own church during a robbery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: Chris Gatlin spent 17 months in jail for a crime that an artificial intelligence program said he committed, only to be freed after the prosecutor learned there was no real evidence.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KRON4

item.title: Decorated pilot Harry Stewart, Jr., one of the last surviving Tuskegee Airmen, dies at 100
item.description: Retired Lt. Col. Harry Stewart Jr, a decorated World War II pilot who broke racial barriers as a Tuskegee Airmen and earned honors for his combat heroism, has died. He was 100.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect arrested after shooting at Ohio cosmetics facility leaves 1 dead, 5 injured
item.description: Officials announced that Foster had been arrested by U.S. Marshalls after a manhunt that stretched through the night.
item.dc:creator: Brett Mills, Fox 5

item.title: Decorated pilot Harry Stewart, Jr., one of the last surviving Tuskegee Airmen, dies at 100
item.description: Retired Lt. Col. Harry Stewart Jr, a decorated World War II pilot who broke racial barriers as a Tuskegee Airmen and earned honors for his combat heroism, has died. He was 100. Stewart was one of the last surviving combat pilots of the famed 332nd Fighter Group also known as the Tuskegee Airmen.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USAID direct hires put on leave worldwide, except those deemed essential
item.description: The Trump administration is placing U.S. Agency for International Development direct-hire staffers around the world on leave, except those deemed essential.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9
